page: Amazon Web Services r5n.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance. It features 96 vCPUs powered by 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware allocation is fully dedicated, supported by 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, which translates to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 35 MB and supports AVX-512 instructions, including AVX512_VNNI, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. No local storage is bundled, and there are no GPUs.

This instance is designed for high-throughput networking and memory-intensive workloads, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 4545.42 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 224.16 GB/sec. Write bandwidth for large blocks is measured at 97.5 GB/sec. These memory capabilities are reflected in a strong PassMark memory score of 2773.9.

Compute performance is characterized by a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 82012.2 and a PassMark CPU score of 57382.97, though single-core performance remains in the average tier with a stress-ng single-core score of 1456.89. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, scoring 22199.72 on PassMark, and achieves 77.09 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, the lack of hardware accelerators limits its utility for LLM text generation, where it registers poor performance of 5.15 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high memory density, and 100 Gbps network baseline, the r5n.metal is highly cost-efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance computing workloads, and enterprise applications requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. It is particularly suited for Redis deployments, database operations, and static web serving, while being less optimal for active LLM text generation or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is built for high-throughput, memory-intensive workloads. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 57382.97, and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 4545.42 GB/sec for cached reads. It excels in database operations and Redis performance, achieving 77.09 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of hardware acceleration. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, data-intensive enterprise applications, and network-heavy workloads requiring dedicated bare-metal resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed networking for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors at 3.1 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core compute and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks
Excellent fit for in-memory databases, achieving 77.09 million Redis SET operations per second
Bare-metal architecture provides direct hardware access with zero virtualization overhead
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, making it less suitable for LLM text generation
